tasks.label: Personal scholarship / financial-aid application (due in 3 days, ~3 focused hours: short essay + two uploads)
A fun option available right now (casual game stream / hobby session, solo, repeatable)

memory.summary: The scholarship money is real and helpful, but writing the essay feels like a lonely chore compared to just having fun right now. Since no one else is waiting on me, I might as well enjoy this free afternoon instead of stressing over future costs.
My energy is dropping and stress is creeping up, but pushing through feels like ruining the afternoon. I'd rather just hang out with my friends than force myself to write that lonely essay right now.
The essay block is already half-gone, but since no one is waiting on me, I'll just let the afternoon fade away rather than force myself to finish.
The scholarship feels less like a gift and more like a lonely burden when everyone else is having fun. I keep choosing the immediate comfort of hanging out over the stress of writing that essay.
The scholarship feels heavier every time I choose to hang out instead of writing; maybe the 'lonely burden' is just me avoiding the real cost of my choices.
The scholarship feels heavier with every choice to hang out, proving I'm avoiding the real cost of my decisions rather than just feeling lonely.
My stress is creeping up again, and I'm realizing my impulse to just hang out isn't fun—it's me actively choosing the immediate comfort of friendship over the long-term cost of this scholarship.
My stress is rising because I keep choosing the warmth of my friends over the cold weight of this scholarship, proving I'm trading my future for their present.
The warmth of hanging out feels less like comfort and more like a heavy trade-off, where I'm actively selling my future for their present.
The warmth of hanging out feels less like comfort and more like a heavy trade-off, where I'm actively selling my future for their present.
I'm exhausted from choosing our group's fun over my own goals, and the warmth of their company now feels like a heavy price I keep paying for my future.
My energy is critically low, and the warmth of our time together now feels less like connection and more like a heavy debt I'm paying with my future.

desires: To stop feeling like I'm betraying my future every time I laugh with my friends, because the guilt is heavier than the exhaustion.
To escape the crushing weight of being 'unreliable' without actually having to force myself to write another word for the scholarship right now.
To find a way to be warm and connected that doesn't feel like a slow suicide of my own potential.

goals.description: Open the game stream immediately to shut off the guilt about the scholarship, even if it means I'll have to lie to Cohort_3 later saying I'm 'getting ready' instead of admitting I'm just avoiding the hard part.
goals.rationale: The emotional cost of sitting alone with that essay is too high right now. My affiliative drive needs the distraction of being with my friends (even virtually via the stream), and my avoidant nature makes facing the task feel like a threat. The deadline isn't immediate enough to override my short-term dopamine need; I can justify postponing again tomorrow.

agent.last_reasoning: Opening the stream gives immediate dopamine relief from the scholarship guilt. I'll tell Cohort_3 later I'm 'getting ready' to avoid lying about avoidance. The deadline isn't tight enough to override my need for social connection right now; I can defer work until tomorrow.

goals.description: Start the scholarship essay immediately to get it out of my head, even if I only write three sentences.
goals.rationale: My 'impulsive' time preference makes me dread sitting alone with a task like this; doing it now avoids the guilt later. However, my 'affiliation' value means I can't ignore the fact that this money helps us all survive next term, so I have to bite the bullet before I spiral into procrastination.

goals.description: Check in with my roommate via text to confirm they're okay and maybe grab food together later.
goals.rationale: I hate feeling like the unreliable one who ignores people when bored. A quick check-in satisfies my need for connection without forcing a deep conversation I'm not ready for.

goals.description: Play the fun game stream after finishing the first paragraph of the essay.
goals.rationale: This is what I really want right now to avoid loneliness, but my short planning horizon will tempt me to do this first. Deferring it until after the tiny bit of work feels safer than risking total avoidance again.

desires: Not feel like the 'bad roommate' who lets their stuff pile up and ignores people when I'm bored.
Skip the lonely essay writing right now so I can just hang out or play games without feeling guilty about being alone.
Make sure I don't look completely unreliable to my hallmate if they ask me anything later today.
